Title: AI shapes more shopping decisions: Adyen report
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Artificial intelligence (AI) is playing a bigger role in how Malaysians shop, with 74 per cent of consumers using AI assistants to discover products, find inspiration and help make purchasing decisions.
The Adyen Index 2026 Malaysia Retail Report found that while AI-assisted shopping is gaining traction, consumer trust remains a key challenge as retailers move towards more autonomous shopping experiences.
More than half of Malaysians, or 52 per cent, said they are uncomfortable allowing AI to complete purchases on their behalf, citing concerns over security, transparency and control.
Adyen Malaysia country manager Lee Soon Yean said consumer spending remains resilient with businesses reporting revenue growth between 2024 and 2025 despite shoppers becoming more selective in their spending habits.
"Consumers are more diligent in how they spend. They are provided with information that allows them to make better decisions based on value, flexibility and security," he said at a media briefing today.
Lee said the rapid growth of digital retail has also raised expectations, with consumers seeking faster transactions, smoother checkout experiences and more personalised recommendations across platforms.
The report showed AI is helping consumers navigate growing volumes of online information more effectively when searching for products and brands.
Among AI users, 84 per cent said there is too much information on social media, while 74 per cent said AI helps them find shopping inspiration faster than other sources.
Some 62 per cent said they want AI to help them discover unique brands and shopping experiences, while 60 per cent noted that retailers they shop with have already integrated AI-powered search tools into their platforms.
Lee said retailers must increasingly adopt omnichannel strategies as consumers no longer follow a linear shopping journey.
"Customers can start their journey anywhere; in a car, on a phone, through Google search, a mobile app or by walking into a store. Retailers need to ensure those interactions are seamless across channels," said Lee.
He added that continued engagement across multiple touchpoints could eventually translate into purchases, even if consumers do not complete transactions immediately.
On AI adoption, he said small and medium enterprises (SMEs) are unlikely to be left behind, although many will depend on technology partners rather than developing their own solutions.
"Affordable AI tools are becoming increasingly available, allowing SMEs to adopt technologies such as AI-powered customer service through subscription-based platforms as costs decline,"said Lee.
However, he cautioned retailers against adopting AI solely to follow industry trends.
The best companies implementing AI are not those subscribing to 20 different AI tools, he said.
They are the ones that understand where their customers are, what problems they are solving and what tools they actually need.
"Just because your competitor is using AI for a certain function, it does not mean you need to do the same. Retailers first need to understand what problems they are trying to solve and what the right use case is.
"We have seen retailers trying to implement AI in only one part of the business because their systems are still not connected. Companies that successfully use AI to improve sales and operational efficiency are those that first ensure their core solutions are integrated," he added.
He said maintaining accurate customer information, sales data and product records is essential before deploying AI capabilities at scale.
The report also found that self-checkout remains Malaysia's most widely used retail technology at 39 per cent, followed by brand apps for payments and loyalty rewards at 26 per cent and AI tools at 19 per cent.
Malaysia Retail Chain Association (MRCA) president Datuk Liew Bin said Malaysians continue to spend, but are becoming more deliberate in their purchasing decisions.
"Competing on price alone is no longer viable in a saturated market like ours. Retailers must look to technology to meet shifting demands and focus on building long-term loyalty by delivering consistently excellent and trustworthy shopping experiences," he said.
The report highlighted the importance of payment reliability in maintaining consumer confidence.
Nearly six in 10 Malaysians, or 59 per cent, said payment errors negatively affect their perception of a business, while 26 per cent have abandoned purchases due to security or trust concerns.
Another 15 per cent said they would switch to a competitor following a poor payment experience.
Meanwhile, among retailers, 97 per cent reported payment performance issues over the past year, while 94 per cent experienced or were targeted by payment fraud.
Awareness of agentic commerce is also increasing, with 85 per cent of retailers familiar with the concept and 51 per cent saying they are very familiar and able to explain it confidently.
Looking ahead, 47 per cent of retailers plan to invest in AI-driven or agentic commerce solutions in 2026.
However, concerns over maintaining customer relationships and brand control, as well as data privacy, security and compliance risks, remain key barriers to wider adoption.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sultan Ibrahim performs Hari Hol prayers at Johor royal mausoleum
Article:
JOHOR BARU: His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, visited the Bukit Mahmoodiah Royal Mausoleum here today to commemorate the Hari Hol of his late father.
Hari Hol is an annual occasion observed by the Johor royal family and people to mark the passing of a late Sultan through prayers and mausoleum visits.
Sultan Ibrahim, who is also the Sultan of Johor, offered prayers and paid respects at the tombs of Sultan Iskandar Sultan Ismail, his mother Enche' Besar Khalsom Abdullah, and his son Tunku Laksamana Johor Tunku Abdul Jalil.
His Majesty sprinkled scented water and scattered flower petals on the royal tombs.
The late Sultan Iskandar, the fourth Sultan of modern Johor, ruled the state from 1981 until his passing on Jan 22, 2010.
He also served as the eighth Yang di-Pertuan Agong from April 26, 1984 to April 25, 1989.
Renowned for his firm leadership and close bond with the people of Johor, Sultan Iskandar passed away at the age of 77 at Sultanah Aminah Hospital before being laid to rest at the royal mausoleum.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Burnham kicks off UK premiership with lucky breaks, investors hope they last
Article:
BRITAIN'S new Prime Minister Andy Burnham appears to have room to juggle some popular economic policies without dropping the ball on the bond markets.
Investors are likely cheering him on so that they don't have to dodge much bigger risks down the line.
After weeks of speculation and intrigue, Burnham was finally confirmed as premier on Monday, replacing Keir Starmer as the country's seventh PM in a decade.
Compared with Starmer's careful — some might say overly careful — stewardship of the economy, Burnham is seen as something of a maverick, someone potentially willing to take more risks to underline his "man of the people" image and burnish his credentials among the left wing of the ruling Labour Party.
While you may think that would spook creditors much in the way that Japan's Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's rise to power from within the ruling LDP late last year upended government bond markets there, that does not appear to be the case.
Burnham has helped himself in this regard by committing to the standing fiscal rules and frameworks and concentrating for now on hot-button domestic issues like devolved government, defence, fairness, health and homelessness.
Even though benchmark 10-year British government borrowing rates remain the highest of the G7 nations, they have stayed relatively calm near five per cent amid Burnham's spectacularly quick ascent since poor local election results in early May sparked calls for Starmer to step aside.
Indeed, sterling has appreciated almost three per cent against the euro since then, hitting its best levels against the single currency in over a year last week.
Burnham's surprise announcement on Monday that former defence secretary John Healey would be his finance minister had little immediate market impact, with Shabana Mahmood — who had been widely tipped for the top economic job — remaining interior minister.
Some think that is mainly because the financial ship will likely be steered directly by Number 10 rather than being guided primarily by the Chancellor of the Exchequer next door.
"We retain our view that economic policy is increasingly going to be directed by the prime minister's office, and delivered by the chancellor rather than formulated out of Number 11," Barclays' Jack Meaning and Cian Hennigan wrote late last week.
But even sensitivity to some of Burnham's specific plans may have been curbed by two big potential buffers that should allow him both wiggle room and time to pursue his agenda without blowing up the gilt market.
First, the Bank of England may have cover to hold off on rate hikes because of moderating inflation pressures, including from the downward reset of the regulatory energy price cap in September. What's more, the BoE is also seeing something very unusual: a drop in UK borrowing.
That relates to Burnham's second lucky break: the legacy of former Chancellor Rachel Reeves.
[[nid:1494007]]
Her tight adherence to fiscal rules that commit to stabilising the country's debt over time may have contributed to the government's poor approval rating, but could also be a gift to her successor because of the comfort they provide investors.
As ING's James Smith pointed out on Monday, this year has already seen a steep £58 billion drop in gilt sales to some £246 billion. That should comfortably cover much of the tax and spend tweaks Burnham has hinted at so far.
Indeed, that fiscal shift may even embolden the former Manchester mayor to attempt a more ambitious budget rethink this autumn.
"Britain is a rare example of an economy going through genuine fiscal tightening right now, a consequence of the ongoing freeze in tax thresholds," he wrote, adding there were potentially bigger budget issues Burnham may feel he can tackle soon.
These include welfare reform, a property tax review, more defence spending, the unfreezing of tax-free thresholds and potentially switching taxation more to income tax from national insurance payroll tax.
But "a boring budget doesn't win elections," Smith added.
And UK elections matter for markets even more than usual because of the party waiting in the wings.
If a timid Burnham programme fails to boost Labour Party poll ratings, then the election chances of Reform UK — the right-wing party now led by Nigel Farage — will only improve — and that moves the balance of risks for investors onto a different plane.
Even after Labour's switch of leader, Reform remains slightly ahead of Labour in the most recent polls.
Reform UK would be an unpredictable wildcard for most investors, and many would find it hard not to draw comparisons with the brief, disastrous right-wing Conservative administration of Liz Truss in 2022.
Her wild and unfunded "go for growth" budget plan nearly melted down the entire gilt market before she was forced into a U-turn.
And, as the International Monetary Fund outlined this month, the lingering effects of that episode still dog the gilt market to some degree.
The IMF analysis into the elevated UK government bond "term premium" — or the additional compensation for risks demanded by investors for holding longer-dated debt — showed that the 2022 episode has increased the market's vulnerability ever since.
"The September 2022 gilt market turmoil seems to mark a regime shift in how shocks are transmitted into gilt yields," it said, adding it was a major reason for the beefed up term premium.
A Reform UK government could classify as a shock, as could a budget twist from Burnham. Investors have to decide which is better or worse for the bonds they hold.
* The writer is from Reuters
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bank Negara to continue promoting two-way flows in onshort financial market
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bank Negara Malaysia will continue its efforts to promote two-way flows and deepen Malaysia's onshore financial market through the measured liberalisation of its foreign exchange policy (FEP) framework, while safeguarding Malaysia's financial and macroeconomic stability.
Bank Negara Governor Datuk Seri Abdul Rasheed Ghaffour said the central bank remains committed to strengthening the vibrancy, liquidity and efficiency of the domestic financial market through a combination of market development initiatives, sustained engagement with market participants and carefully calibrated policy enhancements, including the FEP framework.
In an exclusive interview with Bernama, the Bank Negara governor said Malaysia has progressively liberalised its FEP framework over the years, providing residents and foreign investors with significant flexibility to undertake cross-border transactions and move capital, while maintaining prudent safeguards against risks from external borrowings and capital flows.
"We have progressively liberalised the FEP framework over the years, including flexibility for exporters to manage their export proceeds. We adopt a measured, gradual, and phased approach to balance greater flexibility for businesses with the need to preserve financial stability," he said.
Abdul Rasheed highlighted that Malaysia's FEP framework has been seen as progressively liberal, noting that resident investors without domestic ringgit borrowing are generally free to invest overseas without limits, while foreign investors can transfer funds freely for their investment and returns in line with international norms.
He said that for resident investors with domestic ringgit borrowing, Bank Negara has continued to adopt a facilitative approach in providing flexibility for overseas investments.
"For example, residents can invest overseas for productive purposes with considerable flexibility. Likewise, foreign investors are generally free to repatriate or transfer funds for legitimate purposes," he said.
The FEP framework and policy measures encouraging repatriation and conversion of overseas investment income have contributed to greater spillover effects on Malaysia's economy, said Abdul Rasheed.
Bank Negara also oversees offshore foreign currency borrowings by residents, as these borrowings impact Malaysia's external debt position. This is to ensure borrowers have adequate foreign currency income or appropriate natural hedges to service those obligations.
"So, it is not really about imposing restrictions. Rather, it is a prudent approach to ensure that Malaysia's external debt remains manageable and that overseas investment ultimately generates benefits for the Malaysian economy," said Abdul Rasheed.
He emphasised that Bank Negara remained open to further liberalisation where appropriate, with future enhancements to the FEP framework continuing to be informed through close engagement with businesses and market participants.
The central bank has engaged with more than 200 companies in recent years, including exporters and importers, to better understand operational challenges and identify areas where existing processes could be streamlined without compromising financial stability.
"Eligible companies with a strong track record may register as Qualified Resident Investor (QRI), giving them greater flexibility in managing and redeploying funds internationally," said Abdul Rasheed.
The QRI programme allows eligible resident corporates that repatriate and convert foreign currency into ringgit the flexibility to seamlessly reconvert and reinvest those funds abroad without needing prior approval from Bank Negara for each transaction.
He said the central bank would continue to partner with market participants to facilitate legitimate business needs while ensuring that any future liberalisation remains consistent with preserving financial resilience and protecting the country's long-term economic interests.
Abdul Rasheed said healthy two-way activity and sustained foreign and domestic investor participation have also supported robust intermediation in the onshore market, with average daily foreign exchange turnover rising to US$21.4 billion as at June 2026, from US$19.8 billion in 2025 (US$1=RM4.09).
On the ringgit, Abdul Rasheed noted that although the ringgit weakened following the West Asia conflict, it continued to be among the region's better-performing currencies.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Social visit pass abuse rises as Immigration Dept intensifies enforcement
Article:
CYBERJAYA: The Immigration Department has detected an increase in cases involving the misuse of social visit passes, with hundreds of foreigners found using tourist or social visit permits to work or conduct economic activities in Malaysia.
Immigration Department director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said enforcement records showed a steady rise in such cases since 2023.
He said the department recorded 1,279 arrests involving the misuse of social visit passes in 2023, followed by 2,644 cases in 2024 and 2,630 cases in 2025.
As of July 15 this year, a total of 451 arrests had been recorded.
"These individuals enter Malaysia using social visit passes or tourist passes, but they then work or carry out economic activities here.
"That is an offence under the Immigration Act. We will not compromise and will continue to pursue those who repeatedly misuse these passes," he told reporters after the 104th Immigration Day celebration here today.
Zakaria said the department had conducted 7,105 enforcement operations as of July 15 this year, resulting in the detention of 41,596 foreigners for various offences.
He said enforcement action was also taken against 1,127 employers found to have protected or employed foreigners without valid permits or passes.
"This shows that we are no longer compromising on the misuse of immigration regulations and the Immigration Act," he said.
Zakaria said the offence involved foreigners from various countries and was not concentrated among any particular nationality.
He said while some reports had highlighted certain nationalities, Immigration records showed cases involving citizens from multiple countries, including Indonesia, Bangladesh and Myanmar.
"The cases do not point to any dominant nationality. Almost all countries are involved."
On the Migrant Repatriation Programme 2.0, Zakaria said the initiative, which entered its second phase this year, had recorded participation from 275,755 foreigners from 114 countries as of July 15.
The programme had generated RM137.76 million in revenue for the government, he said.
He said the voluntary repatriation programme served as an alternative to enforcement action, particularly due to limited capacity at immigration detention centres.
"Instead of relying solely on enforcement, we also need programmes where they voluntarily surrender and are offered lower compounds.
"This allows them to return to their home countries legally rather than using illegal routes."
On the implementation of the new Malaysian International Passport, Zakaria said 63,475 new passports had been issued since its rollout on July 1.
He added that the implementation was currently in its third phase, with several passport offices nationwide issuing the new version.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: JP Morgan lifts Malaysia GDP forecast to 5.3pct, expects Bank Negara to raise OPR in Q4
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: JP Morgan has raised its 2026 Malaysia gross domestic product (GDP) growth forecast to 5.3 per cent from 5.0 per cent after the country's stronger-than-expected second-quarter economic performance.
The firm maintained its expectation that Bank Negara Malaysia will raise interest rates in the fourth quarter.
According to advance estimates, Malaysia's economy would have expanded 5.8 per cent year-on-year in the second quarter of 2026.
This was driven by a sharp 8.5 per cent seasonally adjusted annualised quarter-on-quarter rebound.
JP Morgan said the performance marked a significant acceleration from the first quarter, when sequential growth had slowed to a three-year low of 1.7 per cent annualised.
It said the expected second-quarter rebound was broad-based across sectors, reversing the widespread loss of momentum seen in the preceding quarter.
The firm noted that manufacturing remained the standout performer, with annualised sequential growth strengthening to 10.2 per cent, supported by broad-based gains across both technology and non-technology production.
"Manufacturing growth has accelerated in the first half of 2026 relative to the second half of 2025, while other sectors, although still resilient, have moderated to varying degrees," it said in a note.
Based on the stronger second-quarter data, JP Morgan lifted its full-year 2026 GDP growth forecast to 5.3 per cent, above the market consensus of 4.6 per cent and the central bank's official projection of between 4.0 per cent and 5.0 per cent.
It kept its sequential growth forecasts for the third and fourth quarters unchanged at 5.0 per cent and 4.5 per cent annualised, respectively.
JP Morgan said sustained economic strength in the second half would reinforce the case for Bank Negara to raise the overnight policy rate in the fourth quarter, despite easing inflation.
Headline inflation slowed to 1.9 per cent year-on-year in June from 2.0 per cent in May, mainly due to lower energy prices, while food and core inflation also remained subdued.
Following the softer inflation data and a lower in-house oil price assumption of US$83 per barrel for the second half of 2026, compared with its earlier estimate of US$102, JP Morgan lowered its 2026 headline and core inflation forecasts by 0.1 percentage point to 1.8 per cent and 1.9 per cent respectively.
"But from a monetary policy perspective, since Bank Negara's 25-basis-point rate cut in July 2025 was delivered to insure growth amid external headwinds, such as tariffs and later the Middle East conflict, a soft inflation trajectory should not stand in the way of potential policy rate normalisation, in our view," it said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Energy, maritime security no longer isolated policy areas, says Fadillah
Article:
IPOH: Maritime corridors are now a central component of national security as global trade, energy supplies and economic stability become increasingly intertwined with geopolitical developments.
Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof said energy security and maritime security could no longer be treated as separate policy areas, given that disruptions to strategic sea lanes carry far-reaching consequences globally.
He said recent developments involving the Strait of Hormuz highlighted how vulnerabilities at key maritime chokepoints could affect fuel supplies, electricity costs, trade flows and livelihoods.
"What happens thousands of miles away from our shores can affect us in terms of electricity prices, fuel supplies, trade and, more importantly, the livelihood of ordinary people," he said.
The energy transition and water transformation minister said this during the distinguished royal session at the 8th World Conference on Islamic Thought and Civilisation.
Fadillah said the impact of instability in the Strait of Hormuz was not confined to energy markets, but also affected international shipping, logistics, insurance costs and supply chains.
He said Malaysia, as a trading nation located along the Strait of Malacca, one of the world's most important maritime routes, must remain prepared to face emerging challenges.
"Anything that happens in that particular area will not only implicate Malaysia, but the entire global community.
"Businesses will be affected, supply chains will be affected, and it will definitely affect the entire global community," he said.
Fadillah said the government took immediate steps following the escalation of tensions in the Middle East by bringing together relevant agencies through the National Economic Action Council to assess potential risks and formulate responses.
The priority, he said, was to ensure supply chain resilience, safeguard energy security and guarantee Malaysians continued access to sufficient fuel supplies.
"We cannot be complacent. Resilience is about preparing ourselves now, not waiting for a crisis to happen," he said.
On maritime corridors, Fadillah said Malaysia's approach was not about controlling strategic waterways, but ensuring they remained secure, safe and accessible to all.
He said Malaysia had a responsibility to ensure the Strait of Malacca remained secure, given its significance to global commerce, economic growth and energy affordability.
Today's conference was opened by Sultan of Perak Sultan Nazrin Muizzuddin Shah.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MAA raises 2026 car sales forecast to 800,000 units
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Automotive Association (MAA) has revised upward its 2026 total industry volume (TIV) forecast to 800,000 units from 790,000 units, following stronger-than-expected vehicle sales in the first half of the year.
The industry recorded a TIV of 385,353 units in the first six months of 2026, up three per cent from 373,636 units a year earlier, driven by robust demand for sport utility vehicles (SUVs) and growing adoption of electrified vehicles.
MAA's revised forecast comprises 744,000 passenger vehicles and 56,000 commercial vehicles, compared with its earlier projections of 730,000 and 60,000 units respectively.
Despite the upward revision, the forecast remains below the industry's 2025 sales volume of 820,752 units.
The association also raised its forecast for all electric vehicles (EV) sales to 120,000 units, representing 15 per cent of the revised TIV forecast, from an earlier estimate of 100,000 units.
Battery electric vehicles (BEVs) and hybrid vehicles are expected to contribute equally at 60,000 units each.
On the production side, total industry production (TIP) rose 1.2 per cent to 356,946 units in the first half of 2026 from 352,626 units in the corresponding period last year.
National carmakers continue to dominate the market, with the combined share of Proton Bhd and Perusahaan Otomobil Kedua Sdn Bhd rising to 67 per cent, equivalent to 256,304 units, compared with 63 per cent a year earlier.
Non-national brands accounted for the remaining 33 per cent or 129,049 units.
Of the 67 per cent market share held by national marques, Perodua accounted for 41 per cent while Proton contributed 25 per cent.
MAA attributed the stronger first-half performance to a 19 per cent increase in SUV sales, particularly driven by Proton's EV offerings and new model introductions, as well as a 69 per cent jump in all types of EV sales.
However, the association noted that the commercial vehicle segment, especially pick-up trucks, continued to face pressure.
Sales in the segment declined 11 per cent, largely due to the withdrawal of the diesel subsidy for private registrations, which weighed on demand and contributed to lower production levels in certain vehicle categories.
MAA expects consumer confidence, stable financing conditions, new model launches, year-end promotional campaigns and the extension of the New Customised Incentive Mechanism postponement until Dec 31, 2026 to support vehicle sales in the second half of the year.
It cautioned that geopolitical developments, exchange rate volatility and global trade uncertainties remain key risks.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Great Barrier Reef at risk of 'collapse', scientists warn
Article:
AUCKLAND: Australia's Great Barrier Reef is at a very high risk of extreme and widespread bleaching and "ecosystem collapse" linked to this year's El Nino weather phenomenon, top scientists warned Tuesday.
Often dubbed the world's largest living structure, the reef stretches 2,300 kilometres (nearly 1,430 miles) along the coast of Queensland state and is a major tourism draw for Australia.
This month it avoided being listed as endangered, despite the United Nations reporting "utmost concern" about mass coral bleaching and the impact of climate change.
Morgan Pratchett, a marine conservation ecologist at Queensland's James Cook University, told the International Coral Reef Symposium in Auckland, New Zealand, that the "extreme El Nino" posed a significant concern.
"It's not outside of the realm of possibility that the next major disturbance will lead to localised species extinctions... and widespread ecosystem collapse," Pratchett said.
This year's El Nino is expected to break records for its overall strength as it warms surface temperatures in the central and eastern equatorial Pacific Ocean, bringing worldwide changes in winds, pressure and rainfall patterns.
"It's a very high likelihood that we'll see very high severity and large-scale mass bleaching on the Great Barrier Reef," Pratchett said.
UNESCO has been monitoring the reef annually since 2021, when it warned it was at risk of being placed on the list of World Heritage items "in danger."
Hard coral cover across the reef declined substantially in 2024-2025, with above-average water temperatures causing the reef's sixth mass coral bleaching event since 2016.
Extreme weather, land-based run-off, coastal development and predation by the Crown of Thorns starfish were also placing the reef under pressure.
Marine studies professor Ove Hoegh-Guldberg from the University of Queensland said a lack of government action had globally threatened the future of coral reefs around the world.
"This could be the precursor to the loss of humans," he said.
"If you were on a big boat, like the Titanic, and someone comes up to you and says the boat's going down, you bloody well get on those lifeboats and start acting and do stuff."
"We're not doing that. The ship is starting to sink in a really massive way, and we're not seeing it as what it is.
"It is so serious."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Removing any minister is PM's prerogative, says Zahid
Article:
CYBERJAYA: The decision to remove any minister or deputy minister from office rests solely with the prime minister, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi.
Zahid said ministers or deputy ministers do not need to resign voluntarily if the prime minister has proof of disciplinary breaches.
"The power of appointment lies solely with the prime minister," he said after attending the 104th Immigration Day celebration.
"If the Prime Minister has suspicions or receives credible information, it is his prerogative, and there is no need for anyone to resign."
His remarks followed a warning by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim against ministers, deputy ministers, and government agency heads using their positions to attack fellow members of the unity government while campaigning for the Negri Sembilan election.
Anwar had warned that those who did so would be asked to step down.
Yesterday, Umno secretary-general Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said the party
had never used personal attacks
as part of its strategy in the Johor and Negri Sembilan election campaigns.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MK Land wins sustainability award for CSR, ESG efforts
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: MK Land Holdings Bhd has received the Community Empowerment & Environmental Conservation Award under the Company of the Year (Property Developer) category at the Sustainability & CSR Malaysia Awards 2026.
The award recognises the company's strong commitment to strengthening its corporate social responsibility (CSR) and environmental, social and governance (ESG) practices, which have delivered meaningful and lasting impacts on communities and the environment.
The award recognises organisations that demonstrate outstanding leadership in integrating sustainability practices into their business operations through corporate responsibility, community empowerment and environmental conservation.
The recognition also reflects MK Land's holistic approach to creating value beyond property development by balancing commercial success with meaningful contributions to society, environmental conservation and close collaboration with various stakeholders.
The award was received by MK Land chief operating officer Nazri Tumin at the Sustainability & CSR Malaysia Awards 2026 ceremony, which was officiated by Deputy Women, Family and Community Development Minister Lim Hui Ying.
MK Land group strategic communications and sustainability head Shahreen Rahim said the award demonstrated MK Land's belief that sustainable development was not merely about achieving business objectives but also about creating lasting value for society, communities and the environment.
"We are honoured to receive this recognition, which reflects our commitment to integrating sustainability principles into every aspect of our operations.
"At MK Land, we believe responsible development is not simply about building homes and townships but also about creating meaningful value for society while preserving the environment through responsible governance and strategic partnerships.
"Through this approach, we aim to build sustainable communities, empower our workforce and ensure that the benefits created today continue to be enjoyed by future generations," he said in a statement.
He also explained that MK Land's sustainability agenda was driven by clear objectives, rather than simply implementing programmes for the sake of it.
Every initiative undertaken contributes to MK Land's aspiration to develop sustainable communities.
"Whether through community programmes, employee wellbeing initiatives or environmental conservation partnerships, our approach is always centred on meaningful impact. This award is the result of the collective efforts of our employees, strategic partners, government agencies and communities that have supported our sustainability journey.
"As the company grows, we will continue to strengthen our ESG agenda and create long-term value that extends beyond the physical developments we build," he said.
The Sustainability & CSR Malaysia Awards 2026 recognises organisations that excel in strengthening ESG practices and making meaningful contributions to the country's sustainable development.
For MK Land, the recognition marks another significant achievement in the company's sustainability journey and reinforces its commitment to further strengthening its ESG framework, expanding strategic collaborations and implementing initiatives that deliver lasting benefits to all stakeholders.
In line with its strategic business growth, MK Land will remain committed to developing sustainable communities that enable society, businesses and the environment to grow in balance through responsible development, meaningful engagement and a shared commitment towards a more sustainable future.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zahid outlines four mandates to transform Immigration Department
Article:
CYBERJAYA: The Immigration Department must move beyond its conventional role as the country's gatekeeper and evolve into a strategic institution that drives economic growth, strengthens national security and leads digital transformation, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi.
Outlining four key mandates for the department, Zahid said the measures were crucial to ensuring the agency remained relevant in addressing increasingly complex global challenges.
He said the first mandate was for the department to become a facilitator of national economic growth through a more efficient immigration system capable of attracting foreign investment, global talent and tourists.
"Today, investors no longer choose a country solely based on the incentives offered, but also based on how efficient the country is.
"Global talent chooses countries that are easily accessible, while tourists seek travel experiences that are fast and secure.
"Unnecessary delays, complicated procedures and excessive bureaucracy will only undermine the country's competitiveness," he said in his address at the 104th Immigration Day celebration here today.
Also present were Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail, Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar and Immigration Department director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban.
Zahid said the United Arab Emirates (UAE) had demonstrated how immigration policies could stimulate economic growth through the implementation of its Golden Visa programme, which offers residency of up to 10 years for investors and highly skilled talent.
He said the programme had contributed to an increase in the UAE's foreign direct investment (FDI), which reached RM196.6 billion in 2025, making the country the world's ninth-largest foreign investment destination for the fourth consecutive year.
"The role of the Immigration Department is no longer merely to control the country's entry points, but to become a strategic institution that must continue to be strengthened."
On the second mandate, Zahid said the department must strengthen national security and sovereignty amid increasingly sophisticated cross-border threats.
"Threats today move across borders without regard for time. Migrant smuggling syndicates are becoming more organised, human trafficking is becoming increasingly complex, while financial crimes, smuggling, labour exploitation, misuse of travel documents and terrorism threats are increasingly leveraging technology and international networks.
"As such, enforcement can no longer be merely reactive. It must be driven by intelligence, strengthened by data, supported by technology and carried out through closer cooperation among all national enforcement agencies."
For the third mandate, Zahid said the Immigration Department must focus on accelerating digital transformation and innovation in immigration services.
He said future immigration management must be strengthened through the use of big data, artificial intelligence (AI), predictive analytics, digital identity systems, biometrics and smart border technologies to enable faster, more accurate and secure decision-making.
He cited Singapore as an example of successful digital transformation through its passport-free immigration clearance system using facial and iris biometrics.
He said nearly 127 million travellers used the system at Singapore's immigration checkpoints in 2025, a 270.2 per cent increase from the previous year, while reducing clearance time from 25 seconds to just 10 seconds.
"I want this department to become an organisation that uses technology not only to work faster, but also to think smarter, act more accurately and deliver world-class services."
On the fourth mandate, Zahid said integrity, professionalism and humanitarian values must continue to be strengthened among Immigration personnel.
He said while technology could improve efficiency, only integrity could build public confidence, while professionalism was not merely about following procedures but also required sound judgement and the courage to do what was right.
"I want this department to be recognised not only for its firmness in enforcing the law, but also for its professionalism, integrity and human values.
"That is the foundation of an institution respected by the people and trusted by the international community."
Zahid said the comprehensive implementation of the four mandates would elevate the department into a strategic institution that not only safeguards national sovereignty, but also enhances Malaysia's economic competitiveness and strengthens public confidence in immigration services.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Singapore-bound Malaysian workers urged to start journeys earlier
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Thousands of Malaysians working in Singapore have been advised to start their journeys earlier to help ease severe morning congestion at the Bangunan Sultan Iskandar (BSI) Complex.
The Malaysia Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA) said its monitoring showed most Singapore-bound workers were arriving during a single peak hour, between 6.30am and 7.30am, causing a sudden spike at immigration checkpoints.
To reduce delays at inspection counters, MCBA advised commuters to stagger their travel times and avoid the heavy rush-hour window.
Travellers are also urged to cooperate with officers on duty by moving towards open counters further down the hall to balance user distribution and speed up processing.
Those using the MyNIISe digital platform should ensure their QR codes are generated and updated before reaching inspection lanes, as updating at counters delays processing and worsens congestion.
MCBA said digital border platforms would continue to be expanded, with manual counters gradually reduced in line with government digitalisation plans.
Travellers are also reminded to queue in the correct lanes designated for their mode of clearance, whether manual counters, MyBorderPass, or MyNIISe, to avoid disrupting traffic flow.
The agency added that public cooperation was crucial to maintaining smooth, safe, and orderly border clearance at BSI.
Over 400,000 people cross the Malaysia-Singapore border every day using two land crossings.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Matrix Concepts enters Hall of Fame for responsible development
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Matrix Concepts Holdings Bhd has been inducted into the Hall of Fame for The Edge Malaysia Responsible Developer Award, recognising its long-term commitment to sustainable township development.
The recognition was presented at The Edge Malaysia Best Managed & Sustainable Property Awards 2026 on July 20 in Kuala Lumpur.
The Hall of Fame award is given to developers that have demonstrated sustained efforts in responsible development, including creating well-planned, liveable and sustainable projects that continue to generate value beyond completion.
Matrix Concepts group managing director Kelvin Lee Chin Chuan said the induction reflects the developer's commitment to responsible township development and long-term value creation.
"Being inducted into the Hall of Fame is a meaningful milestone for Matrix Concepts, especially as we celebrate our 30th anniversary this year.
"It reflects our long-standing commitment to responsible township development and creating lasting value for our communities, shareholders and stakeholders," he said.
Kelvin Lee said the company would continue to strengthen the value of its townships, drive sustainable growth and create communities where people genuinely want to live through thoughtful planning and long-term investments.
Over the past three decades, Matrix Concepts has expanded from developing residential projects into building integrated townships comprising residential, education, healthcare, lifestyle and commercial components.
Its strategy focuses on infrastructure, education, healthcare and lifestyle amenities to support community needs and enhance the long-term value of its developments.
It also continues to strengthen its environmental initiatives through #MatrixForGreen, a group-wide platform aimed at encouraging sustainable practices among employees, business divisions and communities.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police arrest man, seize RM100,000 Black-winged Kite in Kapar raid
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police have arrested a man and seized a Black-winged Kite valued at RM100,000 kept without a valid license or permit at a house in Taman Sungai Kapar Indah here yesterday.
The operation was conducted by the Intelligence Branch of the Bukit Aman Federal Reserve Unit (FRU) alongside the Selangor Wildlife and National Parks Department (Perhilitan) under Operasi Bersepadu Khazanah at 9.40pm.
FRU Commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Rosli Md Yusof said the 21-year-old man was arrested after failing to produce any authorisation documents to possess, keep, or rear the protected wildlife.
"During the operation, authorities also seized a PVC pipe bird perch. The total seizure is valued at RM100,020, including the wildlife estimated at RM100,000," he said in a statement today.
He added that the case is being investigated under Section 68 of the Wildlife Conservation Act 2010 (Act 716).
"The suspect has been released on bail to allow Selangor Perhilitan to complete its investigation," he said.
Rosli said the successful operation was the result of close cooperation and information sharing between the police and Perhilitan to combat illegal wildlife possession and exploitation.
The Black-winged Kite (Elanus caeruleus) is a bird of prey from the Accipitridae family naturally distributed across Africa and tropical Asia, as well as parts of Europe.
The species is easily recognised by its white and grey plumage, black shoulder patches, and red eyes, typically inhabiting open spaces and hunting small mammals, particularly rodents.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Saifuddin: Malaysia to complete refugee registration by year-end
Article:
CYBERJAYA: Malaysia is expected to complete a comprehensive refugee registration exercise by the end of the year, in a move aimed at strengthening refugee management through more accurate and credible data.
Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the initiative, known as the Refugee Documentation Registration Programme (DPP), would produce a detailed database containing verified information on refugees currently residing in the country.
He said the exercise was being spearheaded by the Home Ministry through the Immigration Department despite Malaysia not being a signatory to the 1951 Refugee Convention.
"Although Malaysia is not a signatory to the 1951 Refugee Convention, we are leading a refugee registration project.
"God willing, by the end of this year we will have a far more credible and granular database containing detailed information on refugees successfully registered under this initiative," he said during the 104th Immigration Day celebration today.
Also present were Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar, Public Service Department director-general Tan Sri Wan Ahmad Dahlan Abdul Aziz, and Immigration Department director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban.
Saifuddin said the initiative would provide the government with more reliable data to support policymaking and improve refugee management.
He said refugee issues had become increasingly complex, with implications for immigration management, national security, humanitarian considerations and international perceptions.
"The Immigration Department today is not only dealing with immigration offences but also with highly sensitive issues such as refugee management.
"These are matters that require a balance between firm law enforcement and humanitarian responsibility."
Saifuddin said while immigration laws would continue to be enforced without compromise, the government remained committed to ensuring humanitarian principles guided its treatment of vulnerable groups.
He cited the Baitul Mahabbah initiative as an example.
The shelters were established to care for young migrant children abandoned during immigration enforcement operations after their parents fled to avoid arrest.
Saifuddin said six Baitul Mahabbah centres had been established since 2023, providing care and protection for almost 700 children aged 10 and below.
"When our enforcement officers conduct operations, there are times when parents flee and leave behind young children.
"Our officers do not simply abandon them. We bring them into our care and place them in dedicated facilities instead of ordinary immigration depots."
He stressed that humanitarian efforts did not diminish the department's enforcement responsibilities.
Between 2025 and June this year, the Immigration Department arrested more than 130,000 people for offences under immigration laws, including illegal entry, visa misuse and overstaying.
During the same period, more than 92,000 foreigners were deported.
"I believe the Immigration Department will continue to enforce the law firmly and without compromise.
"At the same time, where humanitarian responsibilities arise, we will continue to uphold compassion and dignity in carrying out our duties."
In June,
Saifuddin announced
that Malaysia was developing its own refugee database through the DPP.
Implemented by the Immigration Department in line with National Security Council Directive No. 23 (Revised 2023), the programme aims to obtain more accurate biometric data and information on individuals classified as refugees.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Indonesian worker jailed six years, ordered whipped for child sexual assault
Article:
TAWAU: An air-conditioner repairman was sentenced to six years' jail and three strokes of the rotan by the Sessions Court here today after pleading guilty to the physical sexual assault of his underage girlfriend at a rented room on July 9.
Judge Ahmad Faizadh Yahaya handed down the sentence to Indonesian national Mansur Sudin, 29, following his guilty plea.
Mansur, an undocumented immigrant, was charged with physically sexually assaulting his 14-year-old girlfriend at a rented room in a village here at 1am on July 9.
The offence, under Section 14(a) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017, carries a maximum prison term of 20 years and whipping.
According to the facts of the case, the victim's father found his daughter with the accused in the rented room after realising she was missing from home.
Unrepresented by counsel, Mansur pleaded for a lenient sentence, stating he was the sole breadwinner supporting his unemployed wife and young child.
However, deputy public prosecutors Muhammad Haikal Hazwan Md Yatin and Wildan Firdaus Ahmad Fazly urged the court to impose a deterrent sentence, emphasising the gravity of sexual crimes against children and the need to protect minors.
The court also ordered the accused to undergo rehabilitation and counselling throughout his imprisonment under Section 26 of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017 and to be handed over to the Immigration Department for deportation upon completing his sentence.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ex-soldier charged with illegal possession of firearms and live ammo
Article:
KANGAR: A former soldier was charged at the Sessions Court and magistrate's court here today with five counts of possessing a firearm, imitation weapons, and hundreds of rounds of live ammunition without authorisation early this month.
Suhaimi Che Din, 49, faced four charges in the Sessions Court and one in the magistrate's court, all of which were read before magistrate Ana Rozana Mohd Nor.
On the first charge in the Sessions Court, he was accused of possessing a CZ 457 Premium .22R rifle with a magazine containing two .22R calibre bullets.
The charge, framed under Section 8 of the Firearms (Increased Penalties) Act 1971, carries a maximum penalty of 14 years' imprisonment and at least six strokes of the rotan upon conviction.
On the second count, Suhaimi was charged with possessing four air rifles: an Umarex Gauntlet 2, Model X-1, Greyhound T-Rex Bullpup, and Sharp Tiger.
On the third count, he was charged with possessing 21 rounds of 12-Bore ammunition (12-Bore Buckshot) and 300 rounds of .22 Rimfire ammunition (.22RF Long Rifle).
Both the second and third charges were framed under Section 8(a) of the Arms Act 1960, which provides for up to seven years' imprisonment, a fine not exceeding RM10,000, or both, upon conviction.
On the fourth count, he was charged under Section 36(1) of the Arms Act 1960 with possessing four imitation air rifles — a Greyhound AP900, two homemade .22 (5.6mm) calibre air rifles, and a homemade air rifle marked 'Spark'.
The offence carries a maximum penalty of one year in jail, a fine of up to RM5,000, or both.
All four offences were allegedly committed at an orchard in Kampung Wang Pisang, near Taman Beseri Jaya, Beseri, at 12.45am on July 5.
In the magistrate's Court, Suhaimi pleaded not guilty to possessing ammunition, comprising two boxes of Eley Tenex .22 Rimfire Long Rifle (50 rounds), one box of Eley Tenex .22 Rimfire Long Rifle (48 rounds), and one box of SME Ordnance Sdn Bhd 9mm Ball (39 rounds).
The offence, also framed under Section 8(a) of the Arms Act 1960, was allegedly committed at a house on Level 3, Pangsapuri Terinai, here, at 5.30am on July 5.
Deputy public prosecutor Alia Suzila Chek Pi prosecuted, while the accused was represented by lawyer Zuhair Mohd Zakuan.
The court denied bail and set Aug 20 for mention.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zahid backs review of Immigration service scheme after long wait
Article:
CYBERJAYA: Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has backed a proposal to review the service scheme for Immigration Department officers, saying the long-standing request deserves serious consideration by government.
He said the department's officers should receive due recognition, given their expanding responsibilities and the agency's consistent contribution to government revenue.
"I wholeheartedly agree that the request made earlier by the Home Minister, Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail, deserves consideration by the Chief Secretary to the Government and the Public Service Department," he said in his keynote address at the 104th Immigration Day celebration today.
The event was attended by Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar, Public Service director-general Tan Sri Wan Ahmad Dahlan Abdul Aziz, Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Mohd Khalid Ismail and Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban.
Referring to Saifuddin's earlier appeal, Zahid joked that the Home Minister had left "50 per cent" of the responsibility to him by asking him to raise the matter with Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
However, he quipped that with both the Chief Secretary and the PSD chief present, much of the work had already been done.
"What is so difficult about improving the remuneration and service scheme for Immigration officers and personnel?"
Zahid also expressed hope that Anwar would be able to announce improvements to the department's service scheme during next year's 105th Immigration Day celebration.
"I hope that when Immigration celebrates its 105th anniversary next year, the Prime Minister will announce improvements to the service scheme for Immigration officers and personnel."
Earlier, Saifuddin openly appealed to Shamsul Azri and Wan Ahmad Dahlan, who were present at the event, to consider improving the service scheme for Immigration enforcement officers.
He said the proposal formed part of the Home Ministry's efforts to strengthen the department, particularly following the transfer of several enforcement functions to the Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA).
"The Immigration Department is undertaking various initiatives to strengthen its organisation, particularly after one of its functions was taken over by MCBA.
"Among the proposals is an improvement to the service scheme for Immigration enforcement officers.
"Today, we are fortunate that both the Chief Secretary and the Public Service director-general are here to hear this request directly."
Saifuddin said the proposal was aimed at ensuring the department's human resource development kept pace with its increasingly complex responsibilities.
He noted that Immigration's role had evolved far beyond border control to include safeguarding national security, facilitating investments, managing expatriates and refugees, and supporting Malaysia's economic competitiveness.
The minister jokingly remarked that "half the job" had already been accomplished because both senior civil service leaders were present, while the remaining half depended on Zahid raising the matter with the Prime Minister.
"We didn't invite them here without a purpose," he quipped.
Saifuddin also commended Immigration officers for their contribution to national development, describing the department as one of the government's top revenue-generating agencies.
He said the department had consistently collected more than RM5 billion annually over the past three years and remained on track to exceed that figure again this year, having already generated RM2.6 billion as of June.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: India's 'Cockroach' movement vows to protest until education minister resigns
Article:
NEW DELHI: India's "Cockroach" party movement vowed Tuesday to press ahead with their campaign until the education minister resigns, a day after at least 180 people were injured in protests in New Delhi.
Thousands of protesters gathered in the heart of the Indian capital on Monday, demanding Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation and wider examination reforms, in a show of force by the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) online movement.
The CJP has won millions of followers on social media since its launch in May, fuelling one of the biggest challenges to Hindu-nationalist Prime Minister Narendra Modi since he won his third term in office in 2024.
"We won't stop. Pradhan has to be sacked," CJP's Ashutosh Ranka said in a social media post.
Hundreds of protesters remained camped at the Jantar Mantar protest site on Tuesday, many of them calling for wider changes in the education system.
On Monday, police fired tear gas and baton-charged protesters to stop them for marching on to parliament, in one of the biggest street rallies in Delhi in around five years.
At least 178 people were injured in clashes between protesters and security officials, Delhi Police said.
That included 118 police personnel, including senior officers, it said while criticising what it described as "unruly, aggressive and violent behaviour" by a "violent mob."
"During the scuffle, around 60 protesters were also reported to get injured," it said in a statement late Monday.
Protest organisers condemned the "high-handedness" of the authorities and said hundreds of protesters had been injured.
"It's a shameful day in the history of Indian democracy that people, honest students, who had come for a genuine issue were brutally beaten by the Delhi police," Ranka told AFP.
Ranka, who met senior minister JP Nadda on Monday, said the government had yet to respond to their demands.
Despite rapid economic growth, millions of people in the world's most populous nation still struggle to find stable and well-paying jobs, fuelling discontent.
The protests follow a series of examination scandals that have shaken confidence in the country's testing system.
Some 2.2 million aspiring medical students sat for a re-examination under tight security last month after the previous test was scrapped following the leak of a question paper.
That came on top of another scandal related to the online marking system of tests taken by nearly two million high school students.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Indonesia passes bill to set up international financial centre
Article:
JAKARTA: Indonesia's parliament passed a bill on Tuesday to establish an international financial centre, with the resort island of Bali previously floated as a possible location.
Finance Minister Purbaya Yudhi Sadewa said the financial centre, known as PFII, is expected to draw foreign investment and help accelerate the country's growth.
"PFII attracts foreign capital flows and a sustainable portfolio investment as a long-term source of financing to expand the national economic pie so that Indonesia's economy can grow faster," Purbaya told lawmakers on Tuesday.
"If the national economy can expand and grow faster, this will yield significant long-term benefits for national development throughout Indonesia."
The bill, which was passed unanimously, covers the institutional framework for the financial centre as well as tax incentives, according to lawmaker Mohamad Hekal.
An arbitration body and a special court authorised to examine and decide cases within the centre would also be established, he added.
The text of the bill has not yet been made public.
Bali was proposed as a possible site for the financial centre, according to a statement in May from the Coordinating Ministry of Economic Affairs.
President Prabowo Subianto has set an economic growth target of eight per cent by 2029, powered by a boost in public spending.
The economy grew 5.6 per cent year-on-year in the first quarter of 2026, according to the national statistics agency.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bursa Malaysia lower at midday as it tracks Wall St weakness
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia ended the morning session in negative territory, tracking the weaker performance on Wall Street overnight.
At 12.30pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) eased 6.97 points to 1,715.32 from Monday's close of 1,722.29.
The benchmark index opened 3.33 points higher at 1,725.62, and moved between 1,712.66 and 1,726.88 during the morning trading session.
The broader market turned negative with losers leading gainers 492 to 456, while 533 counters were unchanged, 1,254 untraded and 12 suspended.
Turnover stood at 2.10 billion units worth RM1.39 billion.
Malacca Securities said while Wall Street gave up its intraday gains and ended in negative territory, investors are expected to turn their attention toward the earnings season, with megacap tech counters reporting their earnings this Wednesday.
The brokerage said crude palm oil (CPO) futures are projected to experience stronger price momentum moving forward, hence plantation stocks such as Kuala Lumpur Kepong, United Plantations and TH Plantations are favoured. "The ongoing El Niño weather and geopolitical conflict lifting crude oil prices is expected to benefit the CPO sector," it added.
Among the heavyweight counters, Maybank fell two sen to RM11, Public Bank dropped one sen to RM5.18, CIMB fell eight sen to RM7.67, Tenaga Nasional gained two sen to RM14.32, and IHH Healthcare was flat at RM8.50.
Active stocks included Aimax, which edged up half a sen to 1.5 sen, while Main Market debutant Stratus Global surged RM1.20 to RM2. VS Industry added 2.5 sen to 25 sen, Zetrix AI inched up a sen to 73 sen, but Velesto Energy slipped half a sen to 25 sen.
Top gainers were Malaysian Pacific Industries, which went up RM2.42 to RM47.08, while Nestle advanced RM1.34 to RM94, and  Mi Technovation rallied 91 sen to RM5.78. Pentamaster jumped 29 sen to RM5.20, and UWC bounced 27 sen to RM6.17.
United Plantations led decliners, slipping 54 sen to RM34.06, followed by Kuala Lumpur Kepong, which lost 46 sen to RM21.12. Petronas Gas fell 22 sen to RM17.40, Petronas Dagangan shed 16 sen to RM19.50, and Far East was 14 sen lower at RM3.80.
Among the broader indices, the FBM Emas Index dropped 24.39 points to 12,676.79, the FBM Top 100 Index slipped 28.38 points to 12,503.77, and the FBM Emas Shariah Index reduced 19.79 points to 12,509.86.
The FBM Mid 70 Index climbed 54.85 points to 17,969.74, while the FBM ACE Index advanced 32.04 points to 4,942.81.
By sector, the Financial Services Index lost 51.86 points to 20,246.31, while the Energy Index fell 3.32 points to 772.44, and the Industrial Products and Services Index eased 0.60 of a point to 186.59.
The Plantation Index shed 130.86 points to 9,362.68.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: China summons Philippine ambassador over South China Sea encounter
Article:
BEIJING: Beijing said it summoned the Philippine ambassador to China on Tuesday over an encounter a day earlier between the two countries in the Second Thomas Shoal, according to a statement from its foreign ministry.
China and the Philippines traded accusations on Monday after an encounter between their vessels in contested South China Sea waters, with Manila saying one of its navy personnel was struck on the head and Beijing saying Manila provoked the incident.
"The Philippine side provoked the incident first, yet has turned around and made counter-accusations, distorted the facts and engaged in malicious hype," the Chinese foreign ministry said.
The China Coast Guard (CCG) said on Monday two rubber boats sent by the Philippines' grounded vessel BRP Sierra Madre rammed into a Chinese patrol boat in the South China Sea and the Philippine personnel initiated "malicious attacks" against Chinese law enforcement officers by using oars and long sticks.
China's foreign ministry called the actions taken by the Philippines "egregious in nature."
Manila previously accused Beijing of aggressively striking one of its navy staff on the head with a wooden baton during Monday's encounter and said one navy rubber boat was damaged.
In a separate statement on Tuesday, the CCG said China permitted the Philippine side to transfer the injured personnel through small shuttle boats from the grounded Philippine warship "out of humanitarian consideration."
"The CCG inquired and verified the Philippine boats, and monitored the whole process," CCG spokesperson Jiang Lue said, adding China will continue to carry out "rights-protection and law-enforcement operations" in waters near the Second Thomas Shoal, the contested area.
The Philippines embassy in Beijing did not immediately respond to a request for comment on the CCG and Chinese foreign ministry statements.
The Philippine military on Tuesday called China's claims "false and misleading", saying its personnel did not initiate any confrontation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Forest City: Network School, other tenants bound by Malaysian law
Article:
ISKANDAR PUTERI: Forest City says no tenant or operator within the development is exempt from Malaysian laws, regulatory oversight or licensing requirements.
Master developer Country Garden Pacificview Sdn Bhd said it welcomed the government's findings on the investigation involving Network School.
It said it had cooperated fully with the authorities from the outset and appreciated the government's swift handling of the matter.
The company also welcomed Digital Minister Gobind Singh Deo's position that any alleged breach of the law or licensing requirements must be thoroughly investigated, with appropriate action taken if wrongdoing is established.
Gobind had said any breach of licensing conditions or Malaysian laws would be thoroughly investigated, with enforcement action taken where necessary.
"Forest City has always operated in strict accordance with Malaysian laws, government policies and regulatory requirements. No tenant, operator or organisation is above the law," it said in a statement.
"Any matter involving licensing, immigration or regulatory compliance must be determined through due process by the relevant authorities."
The developer said Network School was among the organisations attracted to Forest City's investment ecosystem, but added that it remained subject to the same regulatory oversight as every other investor, tenant and operator.
It said Forest City neither granted regulatory exemptions nor assumed the role of enforcement agencies.
The company said it had a zero-tolerance stance against any breach of the law, licensing conditions, immigration regulations or misuse of premises.
It said it would exercise its legal and contractual rights if investigations established any breach involving legal, licensing, immigration, land use or contractual obligations.
Forest City said its primary role remained to support the Johor government's vision of developing the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ) and Forest City Special Financial Zone (SFZ) into globally competitive hubs for high-value investments, digital innovation and emerging industries.
It said it was confident the outcome of the investigations would
strengthen public and investor confidence in the SFZ and help attract more global information technology and artificial intelligence companies to Johor.
The issue came under the spotlight after concerns were raised over the operations of Network School, an international technology-focused learning community that established a presence in Forest City.
This prompted investigations by several government agencies into compliance with licensing, immigration and regulatory requirements.
Earlier, Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said the state would not allow any organisation to use Johor as a base to spread ideologies contrary to the law or undermine the sovereignty and interests of Johor and Malaysia.
He also called for investigations into claims that Israelis had joined the Network School community using second passports.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 19 foreigners linked to Network School remain untraced, says Immigration Dept
Article:
CYBERJAYA: Nineteen foreigners linked to the Network School in Forest City, Johor, have yet to be traced, with the Immigration Department investigating whether they remain in Malaysia or have left the country illegally.
Immigration Department director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said the individuals were among 430 foreigners identified as being linked to activities involving the group.
He said 210 of them were still in Malaysia, while 201 had been confirmed to have left the country.
"The remaining 19 cannot be traced. They may have left through illegal routes or may have entered the country illegally.
"We will conduct further investigations to determine whether they are still in Malaysia or have already left," he told reporters after the 104th Immigration Day celebration here today.
Zakaria said the department had conducted two inspections at the location in cooperation with other enforcement agencies, state authorities and local councils, but had yet to find evidence of the alleged activities.
"We carried out inspections involving 379 foreigners who were present at the Network School.
"Based on our data, the total number of individuals linked to Network School activities is 430.
"Of this number, 210 remain in Malaysia, 201 have been detected to have left the country, and 19 cannot be traced," he said.
He said the Immigration Department would continue working with relevant agencies to establish the status and whereabouts of the remaining individuals.
On claims that individuals linked to the Network School may have been holding multiple passports, Zakaria said the department had not found any evidence of such cases so far.
He said the individuals were using valid passports issued by their respective countries, adding that the immigration system could only detect multiple passport usage if a person entered or exited Malaysia using different passports.
"If someone enters using a passport from one country and later exits using another passport, the system will automatically detect it through biometric records and other checks.
"However, if the person consistently uses only one passport, even if we suspect they may have another passport, we cannot identify it through our system," he said.
On July 18, Johor Regent Tunku Ismail Sultan Ibrahim had said that the state government and local authorities would conduct a
thorough investigation
into the issue involving the Network School.
His Royal Highness said the state government and local councils would look into matters under their respective jurisdictions, while issues involving the entry of foreigners into Malaysia fell under the purview of the Home Ministry.
Concerns were previously raised over the operations of the Network School in Forest City involving Israeli nationals.
Tunku Ismail said any action taken would be based on investigation findings and compliance with existing laws and regulations.
Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi had previously said a
Stop-Work Notice
had been issued to premises linked to the Network School's operations in Forest City.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: IRC discussions will shape future AI laws, says Fahmi
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Discussions at the 2026 International Regulatory Conference (IRC 2026) will be translated into legislation, with the Online Safety Act serving as a model for how policy deliberations can lead to legal reforms, Communications Minister Datuk Seri Fahmi Fadzil said.
Fahmi said the government, through several committees, had drafted and subsequently tabled the Act in Parliament following earlier rounds of discussions. A similar process is now underway to develop a legal framework for artificial intelligence (AI).
"Many of the discussions on these emerging technologies will be taken forward and discussed either at the Cabinet level or among the secretaries-general so that existing laws can be improved or new bills drafted before being tabled in the Dewan Rakyat," he told reporters.
He was speaking at a press conference after officiating the two-day conference.
Fahmi said the Digital Ministry was leading efforts to draft the AI legal framework, although he acknowledged the process would take time.
In the meantime, he said regulators, including the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC), would continue monitoring developments and intervene where necessary.
He cited the misuse of Grok AI to generate inappropriate images as an example of how regulators could act even without a dedicated legal framework, noting that MCMC had engaged X to implement guardrails, which have since been put in place.
"We don't want to signal that we are against technology or investment. We want technology and investment that not only bring benefits but also prevent harm. That is the message we want to convey," he said.
Fahmi said findings from IRC 2026 would also be presented to the International Telecommunication Union (ITU), of which Malaysia is a council member, to facilitate comparisons of regulatory frameworks across jurisdictions.
He said the aim was to ensure regulatory frameworks kept pace with technological advances without discouraging investment in emerging sectors.
"What we find happening lately is that technology often moves too fast, creating uncertainty, problems and even crimes that may victimise users. That is why we see a platform like the IRC as being timely," he said.
The Online Safety Act, which was
passed by the Dewan Rakyat
on Dec 11, 2024, came into force on Jan 1 this year.
The Act requires social media platform providers to fulfil three key responsibilities: ensuring platform safety, protecting children under 13, and restricting access to harmful content.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Platforms cooperate but seek more time for under-16 social media ban
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Social media platforms are cooperating with Malaysia's ban on users under 16 but have requested additional time to fully comply, Communications Minister Datuk Seri Fahmi Fadzil said.
Fahmi said the platforms were engaging with the government to ensure they fully understood the requirements under the Risk Mitigation Code (RMC) and Child Protection Code (CPC).
The minister said the government had granted their request for additional time as discussions were still ongoing.
"We see good signs among the platforms. They want to make sure they do not misunderstand what is intended, which is why they have asked for a little more time.
"We have granted it, and I see that the cooperation at this time is good," he told reporters after launching the 2026 International Regulatory Conference (IRC 2026).
Fahmi said non-compliance with the gazetted codes could result in court action and fines of up to RM10 million, or other substantial financial penalties.
"They know and understand. Right now, they want to give their cooperation," he said.
When asked how long the additional period granted to platforms would last, Fahmi said he would leave it to the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC), as discussions were still ongoing.
Malaysia began enforcing a ban on social media account registration for users under 16 on June 1 under the CPC and RMC, two regulatory codes introduced under the Online Safety Act.
The CPC requires licensed platforms to implement child-safety-by-design measures, while the RMC requires platforms to adopt proactive safeguards.
The rules apply to all licensed social media platforms with at least eight million users in Malaysia, which must prevent users under 16 from registering new accounts. Existing account holders are also required to verify their identities to remain eligible to access the platforms.
Platforms that fail to comply with the Act face fines of up to RM10 million.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Son of Brazil's former President Bolsonaro secures US green card
Article:
SAO PAULO: Former Brazilian lawmaker Eduardo Bolsonaro, son of former President Jair Bolsonaro and an ally of US President Donald Trump, told Reuters on Monday that he received permanent US residency, often called a green card.
The younger Bolsonaro, who has lived in the US since early 2025 and was recently sentenced to prison in his home country, said by telephone that residency was granted under the EB-1A category, for individuals with extraordinary abilities in areas such as science, the arts, business and sports.
The process, he said, started about a year ago. Bolsonaro has cultivated ties with US conservatives and the Trump administration.
He has been accused of lobbying against his father's conviction in Brazil for attempting to overturn the results of the 2022 election, in which the elder Bolsonaro was defeated by President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva.
The former president was sentenced to more than 27 years in prison for plotting a military coup.
Eduardo Bolsonaro said he moved to protect himself from what he called unjust persecution by Brazil's Supreme Court, not help his father.
In June, the court sentenced Eduardo Bolsonaro to four years and two months in prison after finding him guilty of soliciting US interference in his father's trial.
Political opponents of Eduardo and his brother, presidential hopeful Senator Flavio Bolsonaro, have accused the brothers of working against Brazil in the US, which critics say may have led to US tariffs on certain Brazilian goods.
A 25 per cent tariff on select Brazilian imports is set to take effect Wednesday.
Both Eduardo and Flavio Bolsonaro have denied asking the Trump administration to levy tariffs on Brazilian exports.
Eduardo Bolsonaro, who was already authorised to work in the U.S. before receiving a green card, said he will only return to Brazil if granted amnesty.
"At least here I am free," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] RM19.8mil cannabis haul bound for Europe seized at KLIA, seven arrested
Article:
SHAH ALAM: Police have crippled six drug trafficking attempts through Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA), seizing cannabis buds worth RM19.82 million believed destined for Europe.
Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said operations conducted between July 11 and 17 by the KLIA Narcotics Crime Investigation Division, with assistance from Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd (MAHB) auxiliary police, led to the arrest of seven suspects.
The suspects comprise a local woman, four foreign men, and two foreign women.
He said investigators believe the suspects were recruited by international drug syndicates to act as drug mules, transporting cannabis concealed in their luggage on flights bound for Europe.
"Initial investigations found the syndicates recruited couriers to carry the drugs overseas, with each mule being paid between RM5,000 and RM15,000 for every successful trip," he said at a press conference today.
Shazeli said police seized a total of 247.8kg of cannabis buds packed in 393 plastic packages hidden inside 14 suitcases, with the drugs estimated to be worth RM19.82 million.
